response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has strong software engineering leadership and distributed-systems experience, including event-driven architectures and scalable microservices. However, his resume lacks direct evidence of building multi-agent AI systems in production, fintech-specific domain expertise (e.g., credit risk, working-capital management), or leadership experience at the scale (team size >30) required for this CTO role. His recent work in AI-agent infrastructure (MCP, LangGraph) is promising but not yet demonstrated at the executive level.
strengths: Strong distributed-systems and event-driven architecture expertise (e.g., GISCollective, Dunnhumby)
Proven track record of scaling engineering teams and technical leadership (e.g., Altom Consulting, RIDE Capital)
Experience with AI-agent frameworks and developer tooling (e.g., MCP, LangGraph at re:cinq)
concerns: No clear fintech or financial-risk expertise (e.g., credit systems, payment flows, embedded finance)
Limited evidence of building multi-agent AI systems in a production, regulated, or high-stakes environment
Lacks experience scaling engineering teams beyond ~20 engineers or operating in a venture-backed scale-up context
